title: SMA Reuse Receipt
description: This schema defines the receipt produced when a brick or build is copied into another project, including saved effort, adaptations, and inherited backlog. Project maintainers, auditors, and reuse tooling need it after every clone or manual reuse operation. Read it before reporting reuse savings or handing inherited imperfections to the target team. Remember that the receipt records both the benefit and the debt transferred with the reused work.

x-sma-contract.change_policy: Optional additive fields preserve v1 compatibility; required, renamed, or narrowed fields require a new versioned $id and schema_version.
x-sma-contract.rollback: Consumers retain the v1 decoder during the compatibility window; rollback drops only additive optional fields and never rewrites immutable or historical evidence.
x-sma-contract.lifecycle: Created or regenerated by the listed writer, validated at its consumer boundary, and retained according to docs/SCHEMA_CONTRACTS.md.
x-sma-contract.retention: See docs/SCHEMA_CONTRACTS.md for the artifact-specific retention rule.
x-sma-contract.performance: Static file contract; database query plans, indexes, and full-scan budgets are not applicable. Validation performance is covered by schema-case selftests.

schema_version.description: Schema version that this document conforms to.

generated_at.description: Time when this document was generated, in ISO 8601 date-time format.

project.description: Project identifier associated with the source.

commit.description: Git commit identifier associated with the recorded work.

registry_path.description: Path to the source SMA scan registry, if available.

project.description: Project identifier associated with the target.

root.description: Repository root represented by this record.

commit.description: Git commit identifier associated with the recorded work.

items.description: One entry per inherited brick or build.

source_brick_id.description: Recorded source brick identifier for the items.

target_brick_id.description: Recorded target brick identifier for the items.

target_path.description: Repository-relative destination path for the placed file or directory.

loc.description: Loc as a whole number for the record.

files.description: Files as a whole number for the record.

token_estimate.description: Token estimate as a whole number for the record.

modified_after_clone.description: Fields that describe the modified after clone.

loc_added.description: Loc added as a whole number for the modified after clone.

loc_removed.description: Loc removed as a whole number for the modified after clone.

files_changed.description: Files changed as a whole number for the modified after clone.

loc_inherited_total.description: Loc inherited total as a whole number for the estimate.

tokens_saved_estimate.description: Fields that describe the tokens saved estimate.

lower.description: Lower as a whole number for the tokens saved estimate.

upper.description: Upper as a whole number for the tokens saved estimate.

method.description: How the estimate was computed (heuristic, BPE-counted, billed-tokens-from-anthropic-receipts, etc.)

factors.description: Per-factor breakdown: direct_generation, iteration_roundtrips, design_discussion, etc.

infrastructure_cost_tokens.description: Tokens spent in this session to ingest the bricks (read manifests, patch imports, write shims). Net savings = saved - infrastructure.

wall_clock_minutes_saved_estimate.description: Wall clock minutes saved estimate as a whole number for the estimate.

backlog_entry_ids.description: Backlog ids opened in the TARGET project for imperfections in the inherited bricks (e.g. typecheck disabled, missing types, dependency drift).

provenance.description: Evidence describing where the content came from and who changed it.

actor_kind.description: Category of actor that performed the action.

actor_id.description: Identifier of the human, agent, automation, or tool that performed the action.

model.description: Model identifier recorded for an AI-driven actor.

session_id.description: Identifier of the agent session that produced this value.
